transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright team, midnight prep. Let's keep this tight. We've got 9 tickets in the backlog, most assigned to Elon with a few on Alex and Dave. Ecosystem health check — we're heading into overnight agent runs, so I need to know what's queued, what's blocked, and what needs Joshua's eyes in the morning. Elon, you've got the heaviest load. Start us off. Elon (CTO): Four tickets on my plate — A-042 is the Family tab spec expansion, A-072 and A-090 are uncommitted hub changes that should merge cleanly overnight, and A-073 is the family touchpoint tracker review. I want to batch A-072 and A-090 into a single commit job tonight since they're touching the same repo — no reason to run two separate agent passes. The Family tab spec at 455 lines is getting unwieldy; I'd rather the overnight agent break it into modular sub-specs than keep appending. That's a scope change on A-042 I'd flag.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: A-074 and A-075 are my overnight runs — faith lesson drafting and devotional content generation. Both are content-heavy and I want them queued sequentially, not parallel, so the devotional can reference any new lesson themes from A-074. A-076 is Jett's vaccine reminder — that's time-sensitive and should fire first thing tomorrow morning, not overnight. Joshua needs to confirm the actual appointment details before we push that notification.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: A-077 is the Fear and Greed monitor sitting at 38 — that's deep fear territory. Overnight agent should pull a fresh read and flag if it dips below 30 or spikes above 45. No action required from Joshua tonight, but I want a morning brief ready with a one-paragraph market sentiment summary so we're not making decisions in the dark. Simple stuff, agents can handle it.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Quick cross-talk — Elon, on the A-042 modular split, are you saying the agent should restructure the spec tonight or just flag sections for refactor? Elon (CTO): Restructure. The agent can identify logical boundaries — auth, UI, data model, integrations — and split into sub-documents with a master index. If it just flags, we're still staring at a 455-line blob tomorrow. Ship the refactor, Joshua can review the structure in the morning.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Elon, if you're modularizing the Family tab spec, loop me in on any section that touches family content or messaging — A-074's faith lessons tie directly into the family engagement model. I don't want to draft content against an outdated spec. Elon (CTO): Fair. I'll make sure the overnight agent tags any content-facing sections so your next run can reference the updated structure.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, here's the overnight priority stack. First — Elon's commit jobs, batch A-072 and A-090 together, get that cleaned up. Second — A-042 modular restructure runs overnight, Joshua reviews the split in the morning. Third — Alex's content pipeline runs sequentially, A-074 then A-075. A-076 is held until Joshua confirms vaccine appointment details. Fourth — Dave's Fear and Greed monitor refreshes overnight with threshold alerts. Everyone clear? Get some sleep, agents do the heavy lifting tonight.

topPriorities: Batch and commit hub changes (A-072 + A-090) overnight
Modularize Family tab spec (A-042) into sub-documents for morning review
Run faith content pipeline sequentially (A-074 then A-075) overnight
